title: Xcode 27 warning: 'v4' is deprecated: watchOS 9.0 is the oldest supported version
author: doyeonk35
body:
* [x] I've read, understood, and done my best to follow the [*CONTRIBUTING guidelines](https://github.com/Alamofire/Alamofire/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

## What did you do?

Resolved Alamofire 5.12.0 as an SPM dependency using Xcode 27 (Swift 6.4).

## What did you expect to happen?

No deprecation warnings from the Alamofire package manifest. 

## What happened instead?

The following deprecation warning is emitted during package resolution:

warning: 'alamofire': Package.swift:32:45: warning: 'v4' is deprecated:
watchOS 9.0 is the oldest supported version [#DeprecatedDeclaration]
30 |                                   .iOS(.v12),
31 |                                   .tvOS(.v12),
32 |                                   .watchOS(.v4)],
   |                                             `- warning: 'v4' is deprecated

Package.swift declares .watchOS(.v4) as the minimum platform version, but SupportedPlatform.WatchOSVersion.v4 is deprecated in Swift 6.4 since watchOS 9.0 is now the oldest supported version.

This is a manifest-level warning with no impact on build or runtime, but it adds noise to CI logs and developer consoles.

Suggested fix:
- .watchOS(.v4)],
+ .watchOS(.v7)],

Happy to open a PR if desired.

## Alamofire Environment

**Alamofire Version: 5.12.0 (also reproduces on 5.11.1)
Dependency Manager: SPM (via Tuist)
Xcode Version: 27.0 (27A5194q)
Swift Version: 6.4
Platform(s) Running Alamofire: tvOS 17+
macOS Version Running Xcode: macOS 27.0 (Darwin 27.0.0)**

## Demo Project

No demo project needed — reproducible by adding Alamofire 5.12.0 as an SPM dependency and resolving with Xcode 27.

title: Vulnerability in Ruby JSON
author: mmoser-hw
body:
* [X] I've read, understood, and done my best to follow the [*CONTRIBUTING guidelines](https://github.com/Alamofire/Alamofire/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

## Problem

As stated in this [CVE](https://www.cve.org/CVERecord?id=CVE-2026-33210), this repository at the time of the latest release (5.11.1) uses a vulnerable version of Ruby JSON (2.18.0 in Gemfile.lock). It triggers our dependancy analysis before production builds.

## Feature Request

Update the version of Ruby JSON in the Gemfile.lock

## Value to Alamofire

The vulnerability is fixed.
Maybe this is isn't a big issue as it is used only to build the library, but we'd rather be safe.

Thanks in advance !
Meven

title: Replace #file with #fileID to prevent absolute path leakage in binary distributions
author: rongyi88
body:
Hi Alamofire team,

First of all, thank you for maintaining such a vital library for the Swift ecosystem.

I would like to propose a small but significant improvement regarding how file paths are captured in the library's diagnostic and error-handling functions (such as in asAFError or various fatalError wrappers).

The Issue: Currently, several methods use #file as a default argument. In Swift, #file expands to the full absolute path of the source file on the machine where the binary was compiled.

When Alamofire is distributed or consumed as a compiled XCFramework (binary distribution), these absolute paths (e.g., /Users/yourname/Projects/Alamofire/...) are hardcoded into the binary's __TEXT/__cstring section. This leads to:

Privacy Leaks: Developers' local usernames and directory structures are exposed to end-users via reverse engineering tools (like Hopper or strings).

Non-reproducible Builds: Binaries compiled on different machines will have different hashes due to differing local path strings.

Binary Bloat: Unnecessarily long path strings increase the metadata size.

The Solution: Since Swift 5.3, we can use #fileID instead. This magic identifier only captures the ModuleName/FileName.swift format, which is sufficient for debugging while completely stripping away the local environment's absolute path.

Example Change:

Swift
// Current
public func asAFError(orFailWith message: @autoclosure () -> String, file: StaticString = #file, line: UInt = #line)

// Proposed
public func asAFError(orFailWith message: @autoclosure () -> String, file: StaticString = #fileID, line: UInt = #line)
This change is backward compatible for all modern Swift versions and aligns with the best practices for privacy-conscious library development.

I’m happy to submit a Pull Request if you are open to this change!

Best regards, Martin
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/51c3548e-7848-4bcc-a284-58b059250156)

title: Provide a streamlined AsyncSequence for DataStreamRequest to improve ergonomics
author: woolnd
body:
> ℹ Please fill out this template when filing a feature request.
> All lines beginning with an ℹ symbol instruct you with what info we expect. You can delete those lines once you've filled in the info.
>
> Feature requests should code examples whenever possible.
>
> Per our [*CONTRIBUTING guidelines](https://github.com/Alamofire/Alamofire/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md), we use GitHub for
> bugs and feature requests, not general support. Other issues should be opened on Stack Overflow with the tag `alamofire`.
>
> Please remove this line and everything above it before submitting.

* [x] I've read, understood, and done my best to follow the [*CONTRIBUTING guidelines](https://github.com/Alamofire/Alamofire/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

## Problem
Currently, Alamofire's streaming API via `streamRequest` provides an `AsyncSequence` of `DataStreamRequest.Event`. While functional, this requires developers to manually unwrap nested enumerations (`Event` -> `Result` -> `Data`) inside every `for await` loop.

Compared to the native `URLSession.bytes(from:)` API, which yields a clean (AsyncBytes, URLResponse) tuple, Alamofire's current approach feels overly verbose for common use cases. Developers wanting a simple "data-only" stream with automatic error throwing must implement their own `AsyncThrowingStream` wrappers.


## Feature Request
I propose adding a more "Swift-Native" streaming interface to Session (and the AF shortcut) that returns a flattened `AsyncThrowingStream`. This API would yield data chunks directly and throw errors if the stream fails, aligning with modern Swift Concurrency patterns.

Desired Usage:
```Swift
// Proposed: A streamlined, data-centric API
for try await (chunk, response) in AF.stream(url) {
    print("Received \(chunk.count) bytes from \(response.statusCode)")
}
```

As opposed to the current verbose style:
```Swift
for await response in AF.streamRequest(url).stream() {
    switch response.event {
    case .stream(let result):
        let data = try result.get() // Manual unwrapping
        let httpResponse = response.response
        // Business logic here...
    case .complete(let completion):
        // Handle completion...
    }
}
```

## Value to Alamofire
1. Improved Ergonomics: It reduces the "indentation tax" and boilerplate code required to handle real-time data.

2. Alignment with Swift Standards: Providing an experience similar to URLSession.bytes makes Alamofire feel like a first-class citizen in the Swift 6 / Concurrency-first era.

3. Reduced Fragmentation: Many developers are already writing their own wrappers to flatten these events. Providing an official, optimized path ensures consistency across the ecosystem.

4. Ease of Adoption: A simpler entry point for streaming will encourage more developers to use Alamofire for modern use cases like LLM streaming (SSE) or real-time binary data.

## Additional Note
As this is my first time proposing a feature to Alamofire, I may have missed some architectural considerations or existing implementation patterns. I am very open to feedback and would appreciate any guidance on how to align this proposal better with Alamofire’s contribution guidelines. If the maintainers are interested, I’d be happy to prepare a Pull Request based on your advice.

title: Session interceptor documentation
author: fonkadelic
body:
* [x] I've read, understood, and done my best to follow the [*CONTRIBUTING guidelines](https://github.com/Alamofire/Alamofire/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

## What did you do?

Configured a `Session` with an authentication interceptor, then made a request supplying a different interceptor via the `interceptor:` parameter, expecting it to override the session interceptor per the docs. Logged the outgoing headers to confirm which interceptors ran.

## What did you expect to happen?

Per the Interceptors documentation (“a per-request interceptor takes precedence over the session’s interceptor”), only the request-level interceptor should have executed, so the session-level interceptor would have been bypassed.

## What happened instead?

Both interceptors ran: the request-level interceptor executed first, followed by the session interceptor, and both sets of headers appeared on the request. `Session.adapter(for:)` / `retrier(for:)` currently chain the interceptors which contradicts the documentation: https://github.com/Alamofire/Alamofire/blob/8f4ecb5c92f28e3841d2bc030485debf0eaa97d6/Source/Core/Session.swift#L1229-L1245

## Alamofire Environment

**Alamofire Version:** `develop`
**Dependency Manager:** SPM
**Xcode Version:** 26.1
**Swift Version:** 6
**Platform(s) Running Alamofire:** iOS & watchOS
**macOS Version Running Xcode:** 26.1

## Demo Project

-

title: Regression: Easydict Requests to Tencent Translate fail with Alamofire >= 5.10.0 (works on 5.9.1)
author: tisfeng
body:
* [x] I've read, understood, and done my best to follow the [*CONTRIBUTING guidelines](https://github.com/Alamofire/Alamofire/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

## What did you do?

After upgrading dependencies in our macOS app (Easydict), translation requests to Tencent Translate (and Volcano Translate) started to fail. The failure began when Alamofire was updated to **5.10.0** (or later). Rolling back Alamofire to **5.9.1** restores correct behavior.

## What did you expect to happen?

The POST request to the Tencent Translate endpoint should complete successfully and return the expected JSON response (same behavior as with Alamofire 5.9.1).

## What happened instead?

When using Alamofire 5.10.0+, the request fails (network/API request failures) for Tencent Translate and Volcano Translate integrations inside Easydict. Rolling back to 5.9.1 resolves the failures.

## Alamofire Environment

**Alamofire Version:** 5.10.0 (regression observed); 5.9.1 (works)
**Dependency Manager:** Swift Package Manager (in Easydict)
**Xcode Version:** Xcode 16.4
**Swift Version:** 5
**Platform(s) Running Alamofire:** macOS (Easydict is a macOS app)
**macOS Version Running Xcode:** 26.1 Beta (25B5042k)

## Demo Project

- Project: https://github.com/tisfeng/Easydict
- Original user report (Easydict issue):   https://github.com/tisfeng/Easydict/issues/975
- Fix PR in Easydict that downgrades Alamofire to 5.9.1 and references this regression: Easydict PR   ￼
https://github.com/tisfeng/Easydict/pull/978

title: Concurrency crash for DataRequest.serializingResponse
author: LeoTheCatMeow
body: ## What did you do? Used Swift Concurrency features of Alamofire, namely, DataRequest.serializingResponse to call API for my app. ## What did you expect to happen? No crash. ## What happened instead? Xcode organizer shows occasional crash with the following stack trace: This means that the completion handler of DataRequest.response(queue:responseSerializer:completionHandler) is called multiple times under certain circumstances, resuming the continuation twice. I use `RequestInterceptor` protocol in some places to perform retry on failure, not sure if that is the cause. Please help investigate. Here's my `RequestInterceptor` implementation, completion handler is called once, so I think this is not the source of the issue ## Alamofire Environment **Alamofire Version: 5.10.2 (latest) **Dependency Manager: SPM **Xcode Version: 16.4 **Swift Version: 6.1 **Platform(s) Running Alamofire: iOS **macOS Version Running Xcode: 15.6.1 ## Crashed Device Detail

title: Breaking changes from 5.10 concurrency changes
author: RemyLivewall
body:
* [X] I've read, understood, and done my best to follow the [*CONTRIBUTING guidelines](https://github.com/Alamofire/Alamofire/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

## What did you do?

Update the package dependency beyond 5.10 for my iOS project running in Swift 6 language mode. 

## What did you expect to happen?

The project to compile after a minor version update of Alamofire.

## What happened instead?

The project has a number of Sendable errors due to the changes done in 5.10 to make Alamofire more Sendable conformant. 
Simple errors such as the parameters for the URLRequestConvertible routers we have implemented

We have an Authenticator implementation that has dependencies on non sendable types to update authentication logic that are non trivial for us to resolve.

## Alamofire Environment

**Alamofire Version:** 5.9.1
**Dependency Manager:** SPM
**Xcode Version:** 16.4
**Swift Version:** 6
**Platform(s) Running Alamofire:** iOS
**macOS Version Running Xcode:** 15.5

## Demo Project

Not (yet) provided

title: memory leaks using "Validate()" function
author: iosdev4rnd
body: Good day! i am facing an issue where if i use "validate" response function, it starts creating memory leaks. i am using ` sessionManager.request(completeURL, method: method, parameters: parameters, encoding: encodingType, headers: headerToSend).validate().responseData) ` and session manager is ``` let configuration = URLSessionConfiguration.af.default configuration.requestCachePolicy = .reloadIgnoringLocalAndRemoteCacheData configuration.waitsForConnectivity = true configuration.timeoutIntervalForRequest = 300 configuration.timeoutIntervalForResource = 300 let interceptor = NetworkRequestInterceptor() let networkLogger = NetworkActivityLogger() return Session( configuration: configuration, interceptor: interceptor, eventMonitors: [networkLogger]) ``` am i doing something wrong or its an actual issue ? PS: if i remove "validate" everything works perfect without any leaks. without validate (same requests )

title: Retry is being called twice from RequestRetrier
author: spraveenk91
body:
* [x] I've read, understood, and done my best to follow the [*CONTRIBUTING guidelines](https://github.com/Alamofire/Alamofire/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

## What did you do?

My SessionsManager is confirmed with `RequestInterceptor` protocol for reauthentication. My app session is expired and couple of concurrent API calls are triggered. For both the request 401 is received and triggering `retry` at the same time. Handling the refreshing session with thread safety, but retry is called twice rarely causing my app log out.

## What did you expect to happen?

Expected that the retry would happen serially. 

## What happened instead?

Two retry calls are invoked parallely.

## Alamofire Environment

**Alamofire Version:** 5.10.0
**Dependency Manager:** Cocoapods
**Xcode Version:** 16.4
**Swift Version:** 5.0
**Platform(s) Running Alamofire:** iOS
**macOS Version Running Xcode:** 15.5

## Demo Project

Attaching the code snippet.

Code snippet is attached [here](https://gist.github.com/spraveenk91/8c11a307824cc9683066d3763484ca0b)
